REPLY MEMORANDUM IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 62364716) FILED BY DEFENDANT DEMAKAS PLUMBING & HEATING, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE AUG-22-2018 HEARING ON DEFENDANT DEMAKAS PLUMBING & HEATING, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF DAVIS & JONES, LLC, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION IS DENIED. THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS FILED UNDER THE NAME OF THE PURPORTED LEGAL NAME OF DEBT RECOVERY RESOURCES. A BUSINESS ENTITY IS REQUIRED TO USE ITS LEGAL NAME, NOT A DBA OR TRADE NAME, AND THAT IS WHAT DAVIS & JONES CLAIMS TO HAVE DONE HERE. EVEN IF DAVIS & JONES WAS REQUIRED TO SEEK LEAVE TO DO SO, WHICH IS AT BEST UNCERTAIN, THERE IS NO REASON TO DENY IT LEAVE HAD IT DONE SO AND THEREFORE NO REASON TO GRANT THIS MOTION. THIS IS PARTICULARLY TRUE SINCE DEMAKAS HAS FAILED TO IDENTIFY ANY LEGAL PREJUDICE TO IT BY PERMITTING THE CASE TO GO FORWARD WITH DAVIS & JONES AS THE PLAINTIFF. COUNSEL FOR DAVIS & JONES IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM. (302/HEK)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-22-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Theresia Sandhu, Esq. of the Law Offices of Theresia Sandhu, 408.313.1174, specially appearing for the Plaintiff;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

There being no other appearance, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Demakas Plumbing & Heating, Inc.'s motion to strike the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Davis & Jones, LLC is denied. The first amended complaint is filed under the name of the purported legal name of Debt Recovery Resources. A business entity is required to use its legal name, not a DBA or trade name, and that is what Davis & Jones claims to have done here. Even if Davis & Jones was required to seek leave to do so, which is at best uncertain, there is no reason to deny it leave had it done so and therefore no reason to grant this motion. This is particularly true since Demakas has failed to identify any legal prejudice to it by permitting the case to go forward with Davis & Jones as the plaintiff. Counsel for Davis & Jones is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-21-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. James Paul Green, Tel. No. 1-415-621-1501 appearing for the Defendant;
No other appearances on the matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Demakas Plumbing & Heating, Inc.'s demurrer to all four causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Debt Recovery Resources, LLC is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to all four causes of action. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to clarify what type of entity it is and why it alleges it is entitled by California business entity law to sue for the relief it is seeking.
Order signed inn open court. Judge; Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk
